entry: Clanker City! The big smoke! The city of ten thousand gears!
One small maintenance-bot, one dream, and not so much as an oil can to my name.
Thanks for the ride! I'll catch the return ferry once I'm rich and famous.
Well. Guess I live here now.

LookAt: WELCOME TO CLANKER CITY. POP. 8,01,1.
Population eight-oh-one-comma-one. The punctuation died first.
Use: Maybe a good honest whack will fix the display.
It rained a bolt. In this town that's basically a tip.
It already gave me everything it had.
Move: Maybe a good honest whack will fix the display.
It rained a bolt. In this town that's basically a tip.
It already gave me everything it had.

LookAt: One genuine Clanker City bolt. Legal tender, light snack, structural member.
PickUp: One bolt. Only eight thousand and ten more and I can afford a personality upgrade.
Use: I'm saving it. It's one ninth of a fortune.

LookAt: The S.S. Eventually. My ride here, and apparently nothing's ride anywhere ever again.
Use: The engine made a noise like a dying accordion and is now doing modern art.
Open: The engine made a noise like a dying accordion and is now doing modern art.
TalkTo: The foghorn wheezed. I'm choosing to hear that as optimism.
Smell: Salt water and burnt transmission fluid. The smell of adventure!

LookAt: A cargo crane, frozen mid-lift. Or mid-yawn. Hard to tell.
The operator plate says 'BOOM-ARM BETTY'. Her eye-light is blinking in morse.
O... I... L. Yeah, Betty, we've all been there.
TalkTo: She'd wave back if she could. She's out of oil, not out of manners.
Use: Her joints are dry as a tax form. She needs oil before she'll move that crate.
Smell: Burnt servo grease. A working robot's perfume.

LookAt: Stenciled on the side: PROPERTY OF THE ORDER OF THE WIND-UP KEY.
Ominous AND conveniently plot-relevant.
PickUp: It's twenty feet up. My arms are stock parts.
Betty could set it down, if anyone gave her a drink of oil.
Use: It's twenty feet up. My arms are stock parts.
Betty could set it down, if anyone gave her a drink of oil.
Move: It's twenty feet up. My arms are stock parts.
Betty could set it down, if anyone gave her a drink of oil.

LookAt: Rivet's Scrap Emporium: 'One bot's trash is this bot's entire business model.'
The shutter sign says: BACK IN FIVE OIL CHANGES.
Open: Shuttered tighter than the city budget.
TalkTo: The echo has rust in it.

LookAt: Official city notice, hot off the press:
'DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG. NOTHING HAS EVER BEEN WRONG.'
'DO NOT STOCKPILE OIL. SIGNED, MAYOR PISTON.'
The Great Dynamo powers every bot on this island. Including me, as of today.
So that notice is the scariest thing I've ever read.
Still nothing wrong. Officially.

PickUp: Let me read it first. I'm a maintenance-bot, not a barbarian.
Evidence. Or wall art. Possibly both.
Use: Let me read it first. I'm a maintenance-bot, not a barbarian.
Evidence. Or wall art. Possibly both.
LookAt: \"DYNAMO MAINTENANCE: NOTHING IS WRONG. - Mayor Piston.\"
Nothing says 'something is wrong' like an official notice that nothing is wrong.
The Scrap & Barrel. I can hear a player piano in there, missing several keys.
...That feels thematically significant.
Open: Bolted from the inside. The GOOD kind of bolted. I respect the craftsmanship.
Use: A voice inside: 'WE OPEN IN ACT TWO.'
This town has a very literal relationship with dramatic structure.
Smell: Spilled 10W-40 and broken dreams. So... a tavern.

LookAt: Something glints down there. Several somethings.
The city's loose change goes to the sea. Poetic. Infuriating.
PickUp: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
Use: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
Open: My fingers are factory-standard. I'd need something magnetic. On a string.
